CheckTitle: Ensure sign-in frequency is configured for admin sessions
Description: Checks whether any enabled Conditional Access policy enforces sign-in frequency for privileged admin roles. Without sign-in frequency controls, admin sessions can persist indefinitely via refresh tokens.
Risk: Without sign-in frequency limits, admin refresh tokens can be valid for extended periods. A stolen admin refresh token can be used to maintain persistent access without re-authentication. Short sign-in frequency (e.g., 4-8 hours) limits the window of token abuse for privileged sessions.

Remediation.Recommendation: Create a Conditional Access policy targeting admin roles that enforces sign-in frequency (e.g., every 4 hours). Apply this to admin roles like Global Admin, Security Admin, and Privileged Role Admin.

SourceNotes: Sign-in frequency for admin sessions is a defense-in-depth measure against token theft.
